message: Jake
Fabar
Welcome.
Hello.
You seem a bit tired, don’t you? Is it because of the party?
No, it's not just that party. It’s been a combination of the movie cancellation and several other things.
Hey, welcome.

The director probably doesn't spend many days in this room a year. It's simple enough to be called more of a hotel.
No one except the director should be living in this room. It's tidier than you'd expect from a man's single living space. Is someone coming to clean up?
It really feels like a temporary dwelling, and I have hardly any belongings other than what I need.
They're not placed here at all.
I’m just wearing a sweater over my usual shirt. I haven’t met any other directors, but I wonder if they’re all like this?
Looking at his white hair and white beard, and the wrinkles etched on his forehead, he seems like someone who has led a life full of hardships.
Maybe it’s the fatigue lingering from the party, but his face looks worn out like an old man.
You've really gone through a lot.
That’s something I should be saying. I can understand how the director feels.
No, it’s fine now. I might have just been a bit tired, both physically and mentally.
How long are you staying in New York?
Well, I’ll head back to Boston as soon as the cleanup is done. At my age, I might not fit in such a city anymore.
That’s true, New York is noisy.
Or rather, it’s not comfortable. It might be good for work, but if I were to live somewhere, I’d prefer Boston.
That might be true.
I’ll go back when the cleanup is done; I probably won’t come back here for a while.
She’s really a poor woman, you know. I have no idea where she went after that, and I can't get in touch.
Maybe she went home?
No way. She’s been living in a hotel for about a year now.
Really!? Is that so?
Yeah. So I think she might be traveling. But, I didn't want her to be alone right now.
It seems like she’s opened up to you. If possible, could you keep an eye on her?
Sure, if I can help.
I’m sorry. I keep asking you for favors.
By the way, doesn't she have an agent?
I guess it would have been better if she did, but, you know, with her personality? She doesn't stick with anyone for long.
So, you're alone then?
Uh.
Well, actually I'm doing something like that at my office. But I've been struggling since she's canceled all her jobs lately.
I wonder where she's going.
I haven't been in touch with Gloria. Honestly, I can't imagine her being in a state to work right now.
That's true.
You know, right after she decided to give up being an actress, she picked up and raised Cindy, right? I feel like she won't go back to her current job.
I think I could introduce him somewhere if he were more of a people person, but that... is a bit much.
Does that mean Gloria was able to manage the work just with Cindy's presence?
Of course.
She didn’t hold a funeral for Cindy.
Tony used to be such a bright and good-looking guy, you know? Before he got acquainted with those weird ones.
Weird ones?
You’ll understand soon enough. Or has Tony already told you?
He’s that kind of type, so you might want to steer clear of him too, but deep down, he’s a bit timid.
I can't quite believe that.
Yeah, everyone says that, doesn't they? I wonder why.
I.
I don't really feel like blaming him.
I thought he was coming back, but the movie's been scrapped, and he's being called a murderer, too; it was a disaster, right?
You must have been doing something bad lately.
Ah, that might be true, yeah.
You must have been surprised when Meryl came at him, but I wonder if he was okay?
That guy probably wouldn't get hurt from a knife like that.
That's a pretty harsh way to put it.
He sure had a rough time. But that's the way it goes for both of us.
I think Jennifer is a great woman. She always knows the right words to say when I'm feeling down.
Could it be that you like her?
Well, that's...
No, it's fine. Don't answer that. I want to hold on to a dream a little longer. Right now, I just want something to cling to.
I feel like I wouldn't be able to stand without her encouragement.
That’s true. There was the whole movie thing and the bad luck of others too.
Maybe it's the work of someone who's holding a grudge against me.
That murder case.
It's hard to say she's a great woman for everyone; how about you?
She seems to have aged suddenly.
But I can't say she looks age-appropriate now. She must have been too energetic to begin with.
It seems she's really taken a hit from the string of disasters these past few weeks. It might be good for her to take a break.
The director is an adult, after all.
There’s no need to worry.
The director should at least help Meryl a little. I doubt there’s anyone else who would try to save her.
With her like that, she probably can't work, and it seems she doesn't have anything else to distract herself with. At this rate, she'll just drown in alcohol.
I hope this doesn't mark the end of Meryl's acting career.
It wasn't just about work, my personal life was all about Cindy too. I lost everything at once.
It’ll likely take a long time for Gloria to recover. Maybe leaving New York is the right move.
I feel sympathy, but there's nothing I can do for her.
Even if this gruesome murder is unrelated, it's certain he'll cause trouble someday. He's that kind of guy.
If Tony were to expose the drug and snuff video issues, even Gordon wouldn’t get off easy.
Is there a risk for Tony just because I met Gordon? What if he gets killed to keep him quiet?
Did Guy intend to break off his engagement with Kristy and date Ginny?
It seems he ended up staying with the Nets, but can he really handle a team that locks up players just for saying they want to quit?
If Kristy is the culprit, there’s a possibility Guy could be an accomplice too, don’t you think?
I wonder if there's something going on between Jennifer and the director. From how they acted at the party, it seems serious.
If Jennifer and the director got involved like that, Meryl wouldn’t stay quiet either.
It feels like something’s about to happen.
Jennifer was not herself at the party. What caused that?
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
